title: Empty SQL result (no rows, no columns)
description: Execution of a valid SQL SELECT-query produces a completely empty result - there are no even columns.
poc: CREATE TABLE test (col1 VARCHAR(255), inserted DATE);
select timestamp_to_str(inserted, '%Y-%m-%d'), (select 1) from test order by 1 limit 1;
key_bug_features: Valid SQL SELECT-query returns empty result with no columns
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test1 (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(100), created_at TIMESTAMP);
CREATE TABLE test2 (value INT, description TEXT, active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE);
INSERT INTO test2 VALUES (NULL, 'Test entry', NULL);
SELECT (SELECT 1) AS dummy_col FROM test1 WHERE id = 2 ORDER BY dummy_col LIMIT 1;
